questions.question: Define edge-connectivity of a graph G. Also define what is meant by a minimum edge cut.
keyPoints: Edge-connectivity λ(G) is the minimum number of edges whose removal disconnects G
Minimum edge cut is the smallest set of edges that separates the graph
Equivalently, size of smallest edge cut equals λ(G)

questions.question: State and prove Whitney's theorem relating vertex-connectivity, edge-connectivity, and minimum degree.
keyPoints: State: κ(G) ≤ λ(G) ≤ δ(G) for any graph G
Proof: κ ≤ λ because removing a vertex removes all incident edges
Proof: λ ≤ δ because removing all edges incident to minimum degree vertex disconnects it

questions.question: Find the edge-connectivity of: (i) Path Pn (ii) Cycle Cn (iii) Complete graph Kn
keyPoints: λ(Pn) = 1: removing any edge disconnects the path
λ(Cn) = 2: at least two edges needed to disconnect cycle
λ(Kn) = n-1: removing all edges incident to any vertex disconnects it

questions.question: State Menger's theorem for edge-connectivity and explain its significance.
keyPoints: Maximum number of pairwise edge-disjoint paths between two vertices equals minimum number of edges separating them
Provides min-cut max-flow type characterization
Used in network reliability and routing

questions.question: What is a bridge in a graph? Show that a graph has edge-connectivity 1 if and only if it contains a bridge.
keyPoints: Bridge is an edge whose removal increases number of connected components
If λ(G)=1, by definition minimum cut has size 1, so that edge is a bridge
Conversely, if graph has bridge, removing it disconnects graph, so λ(G) ≤ 1; for connected graph λ(G) ≥ 1, thus λ(G)=1

questions.question: Find the edge-connectivity of complete bipartite graph Km,n.
keyPoints: Km,n has two partitions with m and n vertices
Remove all edges incident to smaller partition to disconnect
λ(Km,n) = min(m,n)
Proof: min(m,n) edges suffice and necessary to separate vertices

questions.question: Explain the relationship between edge-connectivity and network reliability with an example.
keyPoints: Higher edge-connectivity means more link failures can be withstood
Example: telephone network with λ=3 can survive any 2 link failures
Critical for designing fault-tolerant communication and transport systems

questions.question: Prove that for any graph G, κ(G) ≤ λ(G) ≤ δ(G). Also find edge-connectivity of P5, C6, and K4.
keyPoints: κ ≤ λ: Removing κ vertices disconnects graph, also removes at least κ edges incident
λ ≤ δ: Removing δ edges incident to minimum degree vertex disconnects that vertex
For P5 (path of 5 vertices): λ=1
For C6 (cycle of 6 vertices): λ=2
For K4 (complete graph of 4 vertices): λ=3

questions.question: State and prove Menger's theorem for edge-connectivity. Use it to find the maximum number of edge-disjoint paths between two vertices in a given graph.
keyPoints: Statement: Max edge-disjoint s-t paths = min size of s-t edge cut
Proof outline using max-flow min-cut theorem
Application: For specific graph, identify min cut and count edge-disjoint paths
Shows duality between paths and cuts

questions.question: Define local edge-connectivity λ(u,v). Prove that λ(u,v) = λ(v,u) for any two vertices u and v in a graph.
keyPoints: Local edge-connectivity is minimum edges in u-v cut
Proof: Edge cut separating u from v also separates v from u
Thus minimum is same: λ(u,v) = λ(v,u)
Related to global edge-connectivity: λ(G) = min λ(u,v) for all pairs

questions.question: A graph is k-edge-connected if λ(G) ≥ k. (a) Show that removing fewer than k edges from a k-edge-connected graph cannot disconnect it. (b) Find all k such that Cn is k-edge-connected. (c) Show that if δ(G) ≥ k, then G is not necessarily k-edge-connected.
keyPoints: (a) By definition, min cut ≥ k, so < k edges cannot disconnect
(b) For Cn: λ(Cn) = 2, so Cn is 2-edge-connected but not 3-edge-connected
(c) Counterexample: Star graph K1,n has δ = n ≥ 2 but λ = 1, not k-edge-connected for k≥2
